pattern: Moves only ever go from column c to column c+1, so this is a longest-path problem on a DAG that can be swept one column at a time with a small reachability array.
hints: You may start from any cell in column 0, so track, for the current column, which rows are reachable at all -- not just one starting cell.
To reach row r in column c+1, check the three candidate source rows (r-1, r, r+1) in column c and require the destination value to be strictly greater.
Sweep column by column updating the reachable-rows array; the answer is the index of the last column where at least one row was still reachable.

edge_inputs.description: four by four grid with three possible moves
three by three grid with no possible move
smallest legal grid, two by two
every cell equal, no strict increase possible
only a diagonal path works, straight-across is blocked

stress.description: large randomized grid near the cell-count cap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    total = max(4, min(size, 100000))
    m = max(2, int(total ** 0.5))
    n = max(2, total // m)
    grid = [[rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
